content.claim: Kindergarten coverage gaps (31% of municipalities with no kindergarten in 2022) mirror and compound primary-school access problems in the same small villages, governed by the same national framework with enrollment thresholds (8-child minimum) rather than local discretion.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Documents parallel gap but does not establish causal linkage between kindergarten and primary school closure sequencing
Does not assess whether resolving kindergarten gaps would mitigate downstream primary school viability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
